calculation.intro: Upload a 5-section division data file (corners; H/V orientation; owners + percentages; road corner SW/NW/SE/NE; road width). The tool computes where the border between each owner must be — carving out a shared road strip along one long side, then dividing the remaining land by each owner's ownership fraction — and lets you review the result on the map before creating the properties and a group.

summary.percentWarning: Ownership percentages total {total}% (not 100%). The last owner absorbs the remainder.

importance.title: Importance
importance.note: Reflects the subjective value you assign to this item, regardless of time or context. No justification is needed — this is your personal assessment.
importance.statementLow: This item is currently considered to have LOW importance — it is kept for reference but is not a priority.
importance.statementMedium: This item is currently considered to have MEDIUM importance — it is relevant and worth tracking.
importance.statementHigh: This item is currently considered to have HIGH importance — it is a priority and must be kept up to date.

relevance.title: Relevance
relevance.note: Indicates where this item stands on the time axis. An item can be obsolete or historical yet still carry high importance (e.g. a historical deed).
relevance.statementInactive: This item is INACTIVE — it was once relevant but is no longer applicable. It is kept for audit purposes only.
relevance.statementHistorical: This item is HISTORICAL — it belongs to the past and retains documentary or legal significance even though it is no longer in effect.
relevance.statementCurrent: This item is CURRENT — it is actively in use and reflects the present situation.
relevance.statementFuture: This item has FUTURE relevance — it is anticipated to become significant or active at a later date.

provenance.title: Provenience
provenance.note: Records how this item entered the system. Each change is logged in the history below with the date it was recorded.
provenance.statementManual: This item was entered manually using the Add new button — all data was typed in directly by a user.
provenance.statementImageUpload: This item was created by attaching a file (image, PDF or scanned document). The file is stored as-is — the nickname was taken from the filename and the code was auto-assigned. No content was extracted and no manual data entry took place.
provenance.statementTextFile: This item was imported from a structured text file containing coordinates or field data.
provenance.statementAlgorithm: This item was generated automatically by an algorithm (e.g. polygon division or geometric computation).
provenance.statementAiInterpreted: This item was interpreted by an AI model from an image or document via a cloud API — the result should be reviewed by a person.
provenance.statementExternalImport: This item was imported from an external system or database — verify the source and accuracy of the data.
provenance.algorithmNotFound: Source calculation not found (may have been deleted).

tags.title: Tags / Keywords
tags.note: Free-form tags that help you find and filter this item. Enter one tag at a time and press Add or Enter.

crossRef.title: See Also
crossRef.note: Informal cross-references — use these when a connection matters but is not captured by any formal association or document. Type the other entity's code (e.g. PERS00001) and an optional short note.
crossRef.codeHint: Use the entity's system code — PERS, PROP, or DOC followed by the number.

stamp.createExplanation: A stamp is a label you can apply to any mix of people, properties, and documents. The code is assigned automatically; the short description is what you see in lists.
stamp.saveErrorSession: Your session expired. Please sign in again and retry.

applicator.typeNote: Only items of the selected type are shown. Switch the dropdown above to see or stamp items of a different type.

confirm.deleteBody: Delete this stamp and remove it from all items? This cannot be undone.

infoPanel.title: Ce este o Ștampilă?
infoPanel.body1: O ștampilă definește o caracteristică pe termen lung sau permanentă a obiectelor — persoane, proprietăți sau documente, indiferent de tip. De exemplu, o inițiativă de doi ani care implică multe persoane, proprietăți și documente primește o ștampilă specifică.
infoPanel.body2: Spre deosebire de grupuri, o ștampilă poate fi aplicată simultan pe orice combinație de tipuri de entități.
infoPanel.body3: Ștampilele pot descrie și calificatori subiectivi: nivelul de încredere ATT, potențialul unui obiect, prioritatea sau orice altă etichetă de evaluare personală. Este o practică recomandată să aplici cât mai multe ștampile și să creezi ștampile variate — aceasta adaugă valoare și bogăție utilizării datelor.
infoPanel.codeNote: Fiecare ștampilă primește un cod unic de trei litere (ex. STMP-AAA) atribuit automat.
infoPanel.seeAlso: Pentru acțiuni temporare pe un singur tip de obiect, folosește Grupuri.

group.saveErrorSession: Your session expired. Please sign in again and retry.

panels.inGroupEmpty: No items in this group yet

confirm.deleteBody: Delete this group? This cannot be undone.

infoPanel.title: Ce este un Grup?
infoPanel.body1: Un grup reunește elemente de un singur tip — proprietăți, persoane fizice, persoane juridice sau documente — pentru acțiuni și activități pe termen scurt. De exemplu: grupezi câteva proprietăți pentru o evaluare, câteva persoane pentru a le trimite un mesaj, sau câteva documente relevante pentru un eveniment cu o instituție.
infoPanel.body2: Elementele pot face parte din unul sau mai multe grupuri și pot fi eliminate oricând. Când un grup și-a îndeplinit misiunea, poate fi șters — elementele continuă să există independent.
infoPanel.codeNote: Fiecare grup primește un cod numeric unic (ex. GRP-001, GRP-002…) atribuit automat.
infoPanel.seeAlso: Pentru caracteristici pe termen lung sau permanente care se aplică pe mai multe tipuri de obiecte, folosește Ștampile.
